commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ebcf53a41b2a63197034a7110910a5cce73e61b5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional `encoding` parameter to the built-in read tool, a decoding helper, and regression tests for GBK and default UTF-8 reads.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +45. Total +77 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects: CI and source inspection show the patched read path throws because `encoding` is undefined, and Node rejects the advertised Buffer encodings. The linked GBK bug is source-reproducible from current main's UTF-8-only decode path, but I did not run a live Windows repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action and maintainer review are needed because the PR has blocking implementation defects and only mock/unit proof, so a repair lane should not be requested yet.

target.title: fix(tools): add encoding parameter to read tool for non-UTF-8 files
target.body:
> AI-assisted (Claude). All behavior proof below was produced and verified by a human-run command in a real local checkout using OpenClaw CLI.

## Summary

- **Problem:** The read tool always decodes files as UTF-8, causing garbled text (mojibake) when reading GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows.
- **Why now:** Chinese Windows users commonly have GBK-encoded text files. The read tool produces garbled output instead of readable text.
- **Outcome:** Add an optional `encoding` parameter to the read tool schema. Users can specify file encoding (e.g. "gbk", "gb2312", "shift_jis"). Default is "utf-8". Use "auto" to auto-detect BOM or default to utf-8.

## Linked context

Closes #92664

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Read tool displays garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 10, Node.js v22.22.3, OpenClaw latest main, local checkout with the patch applied.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran regression tests via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Test output:
  ```
  ✓ read tool > reads GBK-encoded files with explicit encoding (regression #92664)
  ✓ read tool > reads UTF-8 files by default
  Tests 6 passed (6)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** GBK-encoded files are correctly decoded when `encoding: "gbk"` is specified. UTF-8 files work as before.
- **Before evidence:** Same test with the fix reverted shows garbled output for GBK-encoded files.
- **What was not tested:** Live read tool call with real GBK file on Chinese Windows.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts` — 6/6 passed

## Risk checklist

- **userVisible:** Yes (read tool now supports encoding parameter)
- **configEnvMigration:** No
- **securityAuthNetwork:** No
- **Mitigation:** Default encoding is "utf-8", backward compatible.
target.author: dwc1997

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
